hadiths.chapter: 10. THE LIMITS OF ABLUTION, ITS ORDER AND REWARD
hadiths.ar:
102  - وَّ رُّوِى ارَّة ً لِمَّا ن َّي ْن َّهُّمَّا مِن َّ 
إَّلذ  ُّن ُّوب ِ وَّ مَّن ْ لَّمْ ي ُّسَّمِّ لَّمْ ي َّطْهُّزْ مِن ْ ج َّسَّذِةِ إ ِلأ َّ مَّا إ َّصَّان َّهُّ إَّلْمَّاءُّ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Al-Sadiq {a.s} said: 'Whoever mentions the name of Allah {SWT} while 
performing wudu (ablution) is as if they have taken a full bath (ghusl).'".  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Thawāb al -Aʿmāl wa ʿIqāb al -Aʿmāl, Part 1, p. 15 • 
Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 1, p. 358 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 67 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 327 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, 
p. 423 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 425 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 77, p. 315 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 10, H. 101

hadiths.chapter: 10. THE LIMITS OF ABLUTION, ITS ORDER AND REWARD
hadiths.ar:
103  -  وَّق َّال إ َّن ُّو إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ مُّوسَّى ن ْن ُّ ج َّعْق َّزٍ  عَّلَّي ْهِ  إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ « :
ذ ُّن ُّون ِهِ ق ِى  ن َّهَّارِةِ مَّا ج َّلاَّ إَّلْكَّن َّان ِزَّ وَّ مَّن ْ ي َّوَّص  َّا َّ لِصَّلاَّة ِ إَّلص ُّن ْحِ كَّان َّ وُّص ُّوو ُّةُّ ذ َّلِك َّ كَّق  َّارَّة ً لِم ا مَّص َّى مِن ْ ذ ُّن ُّون ِهِ ق ِى  لَّي ْلَّي ِهِ 
إ ِلأ َّ إَّلْ كَّن َّان ِزَّ .
hadiths.en:
It is narrated: 'Whoever performs ablution and mentions the name of Allah {SWT}, 
all of his body becomes pure, and the ablution performed between two ablutions 
serves as atonement for the sins committed between them.  
And whoever does not mention [the name of Allah {SWT}] will not purify his body except 
for what the water touches.'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿIqāb al-Aʿmāl, Part 1, p. 15 • Ilal al-
Sharai’ Vol. 1, p. 289 • Rawdat al-Wa’izin, Vol. 2, p. 306 • Jami’ al -Akhbar, Part 1, p. 63 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 
328 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 425 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 77, p. 314 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 10, H. 102

hadiths.chapter: 10. THE LIMITS OF ABLUTION, ITS ORDER AND REWARD
hadiths.en:
Abu Al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim {a.s} said:  
'Whoever performs ablution for the Maghrib prayer, his ablution serves as atonement 
for the sins he committed during the day, except for the major sins.  
And whoever performs ablution for the Fajr prayer, his ablution serves as atonement 
for the sins he committed during the night, except for the major sins.'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 70 • Thawāb al -Aʿmāl wa ʿIqāb al-
Aʿmāl, Part 1, p. 17 • Rawdat al-Wa’izin, Vol. 2, p. 306 • Jami’ al-Akhbar, Part 1, p. 64 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 366 
• Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 376 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 77, p. 231 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 10, H. 103
reference.chapter: THE LIMITS OF ABLUTION, ITS ORDER AND REWARD

hadiths.chapter: 10. THE LIMITS OF ABLUTION, ITS ORDER AND REWARD
hadiths.ar:
105  -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
وَّص ُّوو ُّةُّ كَّي َّث َّ إَّلل َّهُّ لَّهُّ ي َّلاَّي ُّون َّ جَّسَّن َّه ً . 
ون ا س ن ا ن  ي صلى  إلزج ل ن وص وء وإجذ صلوإب  إللي ل وإلن هار كلها ما لم ي حذب  وكذ لك  ن ي ي مم وإجذ ما لم ي حذب  إ و ي صث  
ماء.
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {saws} said:  
"'Open your eyes during ablution, so that they may not see the fire of Hell.'".  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿIqāb al-Aʿmāl, Part 1, p. 17 • Ilal al-
Sharai’ Vol. 1, p. 280 • Jami’ al-Akhbar, Part 1, p. 64 • Miftāḥ al-Falāḥ, Part 1, p. 24 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 334 • 
Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 486 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 77, p. 336 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 77, p. 346 • Tafsir Nur 
Al-Thaqalayn, Part 3, p. 270 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 10, H. 104

hadiths.chapter: 10. THE LIMITS OF ABLUTION, ITS ORDER AND REWARD
hadiths.ar:
106  -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ إَّلْن َّزْذُّ ق َّز ِعَّ ق َّلَّمْ ي َّح ِذِ إَّلْن َّزْذ .  
ق ا ذ إ كان  مغ إلزج ل ج اي م ق لي ذورة ق ى  إلوص وء، وي حوله عن ذ إلع سل.
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
'Whoever performs ablution and wipes himself (with a towel) will have a good deed 
written for him. And whoever performs ablution and does not wipe himself until his 
ablution dries, Allah {SWT} will write for him thirty good deeds.'. 
[AL SADUQ] 
And it is permissible for a man to pray with one ablution for all the prayers of the night 
and day as long as he does not break his ablution, and the same applies to a single 
tayammum (dry ablution) as long as he does not break it or find water.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Rawdat al-Wa’izin, Vol. 2, p. 306 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 10, H. 105
reference.chapter: THE LIMITS OF ABLUTION, ITS ORDER AND REWARD
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE LIMITS OF ABLUTION, ITS ORDER AND REWARD
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٠، ح ١٠٥

hadiths.chapter: 10. THE LIMITS OF ABLUTION, ITS ORDER AND REWARD
hadiths.ar:
107 - وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  « 
وإ ذ إ إسي ي ق ظ  إلزج ل من  ن ومه ولم ي ن ل ق لا ي ذج ل ي ذة ق ى  إلأن اء جي ى ي ع سلها ق ا ن ه لأ ي ذرى  إ ي ن  ن اي ث  ي ذة.  ور كاة  إلوص وء إ ن  
ي ق ول إلمي وص ى : " إللهم إ ن ى  إ سا لك  ي مام إلوص وء وي مام إلصلاة  وي مام رص وإن ك  وإلح ن ه  " ق هذ إ ر كاة  إلوص وء.
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"'When a man performs ablution, let him splash his face with water, for if he is drowsy, 
it will awaken him, and if it is cold, he will not feel the cold.'".  
[AL SADUQ] 
If a man has a ring, he should rotate it during ablution and remove it during a wash 
(ghusl). 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Ilal al-Sharai’ Vol. 1, p. 281 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 1, 
p. 357 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 68 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 333 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 434 • Bihar Al -Anwar, 
Vol. 77, p. 337 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 10, H. 106
reference.chapter: THE LIMITS OF ABLUTION, ITS ORDER AND REWARD
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE LIMITS OF ABLUTION, ITS ORDER AND REWARD

hadiths.chapter: 10. THE LIMITS OF ABLUTION, ITS ORDER AND REWARD
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"And if you forget (to rotate your ring in ablution) until you stand in Salat, then I do not 
command you to repeat it (Salat)."  
[AL SADUQ] 
And if a man wakes up from his sleep and has not relieved himself, he should not put 
his hand in the vessel until he washes it, for he does not know where his hand has been 
during the night. 
The supplication of ablution is for the person performing it to say: supplication of 
ablution is for the one performing it to say:  
'O’ Allah {SWT}, I ask You for the completion of my ablution, the completion of my prayer, 
Your pleasure, and Paradise.'  
This is the supplication of ablution. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 281

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 10, H. 107
reference.chapter: THE LIMITS OF ABLUTION, ITS ORDER AND REWARD
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE LIMITS OF ABLUTION, ITS ORDER AND REWARD

hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {saws} said: "Archangel Jibril {a.s} kept advising me about 
the miswak until I feared I might wear down my teeth or make them fall out.  
And Archangel Jibril {a.s} continued to advise me about the neighbor until I thought he 
would make him an heir.  
And Archangel Jibril {a.s} continued to advise me about servants until I thought he would 
set a term for them to be freed." 
In another report, "And Archangel Jibril {a.s} continued to advise me about women until 
I thought that divorce would not be appropriate for them."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Makarim Al-Akhlaq, vol. 1, p. 48 • Wasa’il Al-Shia, vol. 2, 
p. 7 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 5, p. 515 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 11, H. 108
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١١، ح ١٠٨

hadiths.ar:
110 - وَّق َّال مُّوسَّى ن ْن ُّ ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ « :
ق ِى  إَّلْح َّلاَّءِ ي ُّورِب ُّ إَّلْن َّح َّز .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said: 'Archangel Jibril {a.s} brought down 
the practices of using the miswak, cupping (hijama), and flossing (khilal).'.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al Mahasin, Part 2, p. 558 • Al -Ādāb al-Dīniyyah, Part 1, 
p. 71 • Al-Ādāb al-Dīniyyah, Part 1, p. 89 • Makarim Al-Akhlaq, vol. 1, p. 51 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 677 • Bihar Al-
Anwar, Vol. 59, p. 117 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 63, p. 439 • Bihar Al -Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 130 • Bihar Al -Anwar, 
Vol. 73, p. 138 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 11, H. 109
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١١، ح ١٠٩

hadiths.en:
Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim {a.s} said:  
"Consuming ushnan (a type of cleansing herb) melts the body, scrubbing with pottery 
wears out the body, and using a siwak in the bathroom causes bad breath”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 1, p. 32 • Makarim Al -Akhlaq, 
vol. 1, p. 48 • Makarim Al-Akhlaq, vol. 1, p. 51 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 122 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 337 • Bihar 
Al-Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 135 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 138 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 11, H. 110
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١١، ح ١١٠

hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Four practices are from the traditions of the messengers: applying fragrance, using 
siwak (toothstick), having relations with women, and using henna." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 677 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’a, vol. 2, p. 10 • 
Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 135 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 11, H. 111
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١١، ح ١١١

hadiths.ar:
113  -  وَّق َّال إَّلن  َّن ِى  ُّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ ق ِى  وَّصِي  َّي ِهِ لِعَّلِى ٍّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
صَّلاَّة ٍ .
hadiths.en:
The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} said:  
"Your mouths are the pathways of the Quran, so purify them with siwak (toothstick)." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Majmu’at Warram, Part 1, p. 7 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 676 • 
Wasa’il Al-Shia, vol. 2, p. 23 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 11, H. 112
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١١، ح ١١٢

hadiths.en:
The Prophet {saws}, said in his will to Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s}:  
"O’ Ali {a.s}, make sure to use siwak (toothstick) before wudu (ablution) for every prayer." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Wasa’il Al-Shia, vol. 2, p. 17 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 11, H. 113
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١١، ح ١١٣

hadiths.ar:
115   -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
ق َّق ِي لَّ ي َّا رَّسُّولَّ إَّلل َّهِ ، هَّذ َّإ إ َّرَّق  ُّهَّا ق ُّلُّون اً عَّزَّق ْن َّاةُّ ق َّلِمَّ صَّارَّب ْ إ َّعْذ َّن َّهَّا إ َّق ْوَّإهاً ق َّق َّال كَّان َّث ْ ي َّسْ ي َّاك ُّ ق ِى  إَّلْح َّاهِلِي  َّه ِ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} said: " Siwak (tooth cleaning) is half of wudu 
(ablution)." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Makarim Al-Akhlaq, vol. 1, p. 49 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 676 
• Wasa’il Al-Shia, vol. 2, p. 17 • Bihar Al -Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 136 • Bihar Al -Anwar, Vol. 77, p. 343 • Bihar Al -
Anwar, Vol. 77, p. 346 • Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Part 1, p. 364 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 11, H. 114
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١١، ح ١١٤

hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said :  
"When people entered the religion in large numbers, the tribe of Azd came among 
them with the softest hearts and the sweetest mouths.  
It was asked: 'O’ Messenger of Allah {saws}, we understand why they have the softest 
hearts, but why do they have the sweetest mouths?'  
He {saws} said: 'They used to use siwak (toothstick) even in the pre-Islamic period.'" 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 676 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’a, vol. 2, p. 10 • 
Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 22, p. 312 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 136 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 11, H. 115
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١١، ح ١١٥

hadiths.ar:
117  -  وَّق َّال إ َّن ُّو ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
ي َّص ُّز ُّك َّ ي َّزْكُّهُّ ق ِى  ق َّزْطِ إَّلْأ َّي  َّامِ .  
ولأ ن ا س إ ن  ي سي اك  إلصان م ق ى  س هز رمص ان  إ ى  إلن هار س اء. ولأ ن ا س ن السوإك  للمحزم، وي كزة إلسوإك  ق ى  إلحمام لأ ن ه ي ورب  
ون اء إلأ سن ان ، وإلسوإك  من  إلحن ي ق ي ه  وهى  عس ز سن ن : ج مس ق ى   إلزإ س وج مس ق ى  إلح سذ، ق ا ما إلي ى  ق ى  إلزإ س ق المص مص ه ،  
وإلأسي ن س اق ، و إلسوإك ، وق ض إلس ارب  وإلق زق  لمن  طول س عز رإ سه، ومن  لم ي ق زق  س عز رإ سه ق زق ه إلله ي وم إلق ي امه  ن من س ار 
من  ن ار.  وإ ما إلي ى  ق ى  إلح سذ: ق الأسي ن ح اء، وإلح ي ان ، وجلق  إلعان ه ، وق ض إلأ ط ق ار ون ي ف  إلأ ن طي ن .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Everything has a means of purification, and the means of purification for the mouth is 
the siwak (tooth cleaning)." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Makarim Al-Akhlaq, vol. 1, p. 49 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 677 
• Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 2, p. 10 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 22, p. 312 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 136 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 11, H. 116
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١١، ح ١١٦

hadiths.en:
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} said:  
"Indeed, the Messenger of Allah {saws} frequently used siwak (tooth cleaning), but it is 
not obligatory, so it will not harm you if you leave it for a few days”.  
[AL SADUQ] 
is permissible for a fasting person to use siwak at any time of the day in Month of 
Ramadan, and for someone in a state of ihram (pilgrim's state) to use siwak. However, 
using siwak in the bathhouse is discouraged, as it is believed to harm the teeth.  
The siwak is part of the Hanifye Ibraheem sunnah, which consists of ten Sunnahs: five 
for the head and five for the body.  
As for those related to the head, they are; rinsing the mouth, cleaning the nose, using 
siwak, trimming the mustache, and parting the hair. Those who do not part their hair 
will have it parted by a saw of fire on the Day of Judgment.  
As for those related to the body, they are; Cleansing after relieving oneself ( istinja), 
circumcision, shaving the pubic hair, clipping the nails, and plucking the armpit hair.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al Mahasin, Part 2, p. 563 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 677 • 
Wasa’il Al-Shia, vol. 2, p. 11 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 134 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 11, H. 117
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١١، ح ١١٧

hadiths.ar: 119 - وَّق َّال إ َّن ُّو ج َّعْق َّزٍ إَّلْن َّاق ِزُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى  إَّلسِّوَّإك ِ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} and Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq 
{a.s} said: "Two units of prayer performed with siwak (tooth cleaning) are better than 
seventy units of prayer without siwak." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Ādāb al-Dīniyyah, Part 1, p. 72 • Makarim Al-Akhlaq, 
vol. 1, p. 50 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 137 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 11, H. 118
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١١، ح ١١٨

hadiths.en:
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} said about the siwak:  
"Do not neglect it for more than three days, even if you use it just once."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1 • Makarim Al -Akhlaq, vol. 1, p. 50 • Bihar Al -Anwar, Vol. 
73, p. 137 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 11, H. 119
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١١، ح ١١٩

hadiths.en:
The Prophet {saws} said: "Apply kohl in an odd number of strokes and use siwak 
(tooth cleaning) across the width of the mouth”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Ādāb al-Dīniyyah, Part 1, p. 80 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 678 
• Wasa’il Al-Shia, vol. 2, p. 23 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 2, p. 101 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 11, H. 120
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١١، ح ١٢٠
